title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: They believe that the criminal escaped through the back window.
questions.explanation: Tests complex passive structure (is thought to have + past participle).
questions.secondSentence: The criminal is (1) _________ through the back window.

questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs followed by a preposition and gerund.

questions.firstSentence: I only managed to buy the house because my parents lent me the money.
questions.explanation: Tests third conditional for hypothetical past situations.
questions.secondSentence: If my parents (1) _________ the money, I wouldn't have been able to buy the house.

questions.firstSentence: I'm certain that the heavy rain caused the flooding.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect in the passive voice (must have been + past participle).
questions.secondSentence: The flooding (1) _________ by the heavy rain.

questions.firstSentence: As soon as the tennis players went onto the court, it started to rain.
questions.explanation: Tests negative inversion (No sooner had... than).
questions.secondSentence: No (1) _________ onto the court than it started to rain.

questions.firstSentence: It's a waste of time to try and convince him to change his mind.
questions.explanation: Tests gerund phrases (There is no point in + -ing).
questions.secondSentence: There is (1) _________ to convince him to change his mind.
